Status Report
Off Calendar Sep-29-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Status Report
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Continued To May-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Status Report
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Continued To Apr-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Schwartz for and with Harry Rice.

The Court stated that it read the Amended Status Report, that the landscape was different as the income Beneficiary was now deceased and proposed continuing the matter for 60 days for the February 5, 2010 amendment to be properly noticed. The Court suggested Mr. Schwartz undertake efforts to locate the Mr. Hirsch and serve him. If Mr. Hirsch is not located, Mr. Schwartz is to file an Ex Parte petition for waiver of notice in advance of the 60 day continuance along with a declaration of due diligence. The matter was continued to April 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Schwartz that there may be some outstanding sanctions. If there is no proof of service on everybody, no proof of service on the amendment or a request to excuse notice to Mr. Hirsch, sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will commence from April 12, 2010 forward.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Status Report
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Continued To Feb-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Status Report
Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Continued To Jan-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Status Report
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Continued To Dec-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Status Report
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Nov-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Status Report
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Continued To Oct-19-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Schwarz for Harry Rice.

Mr. Schwarz stated that he submitted a declaration, that he had not received a reply from South Africa, that he now had an e-mail address for the Decedent's brother and requested a continuance. The matter was continued to October 19, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A further declaration is due by October 13, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Status Report
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Continued To Sep-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Harry Rice in Propria Persona.

Mr. Rice stated that he did not know if Johan died with or without issue, that he heard a rumor that Johan had a Daughter, but that he did know who the Mother was or who the Daughter was. In addition, he stated that he sent letters to an Attorney in Johannesburg (same Attorney inquiring about Johan's estate), that he did not get a response, that he was having trouble administrating the trust, that the first deed of trust went into foreclosure and that he did not receive payment on the second deed of trust. Mr. Rice also stated that some of the remainder beneficiaries were deceased. Mr. Rice confirmed that he did not sign the document filed on April 21, 2009. The matter was continued to September 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Rice to file a declaration putting all of the above in writing, to send notice of the continued date to Counsel in South Africa and to any of the beneficiaries he has addresses for in the event that Johan does not have a Daughter.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR#12891. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Status Report

Off Calendar Feb-14-2007 Set For Order To Show Cause On Mar-28-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel, or other interested persons.

Noting the absence of appearances for the record and the failure to file the required status report, the Court ordered Harry Rice, personal representative of the estate, and his attorney, Peter Schwarz, to appear on March 28, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and to show cause, if any they have, as to why the required status report was not timely filed.

Judicial Officer: Stella Pantazis, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Jimmy Peralta; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnson.
